narrative.summary: You're on College Street in Little Italy, walking into a room that shifts from dinner service to dance floor as the night unfolds—low-slung pendant lights over closely-set tables, and that sound system pulling in artists who come for the music as much as the wine. The menu leans into small plates—tuna tartare with roasted nori, duck confit tender enough to pull apart with a fork—that won't weigh you down before dancing. Saturday nights are the move, but come early enough to actually eat.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on College Street in Little Italy, walking into a room that shifts from dinner service to dance floor as the night unfolds. Low-slung pendant lights hang over closely-set tables, the kind of setup where you're half-listening to your neighbor's conversation whether you want to or not. By late evening, someone's behind the turntables spinning vinyl through a sound system that pulls in artists and regulars who come for the music as much as the wine.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The menu leans into small plates built for sharing and picking at between glasses of natural wine. Tuna tartare arrives with roasted nori and seaweed salad, while the calamari gets tossed in a sweetish sauce that cuts against expectations. Duck confit comes tender enough to pull apart with a fork, and there's usually a beef tartare on offer alongside nightly specials worth asking about. It's light, ingredient-forward cooking that doesn't weigh you down before dancing.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Saturday nights when you want dinner to dissolve into dancing without changing venues, or any evening when you're hunting for natural wine and don't want the precious wine bar treatment. Come for the tartare, stay because someone just put on a record you haven't heard in years.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Beautiful spot for dinner. The atmosphere is beautiful and cozy. The food is incredible and light with sharing plates that fit a wide variety of tastes. Highly recommend asking what their specials for the night are. The tuna tartare was a standout! This is an incredible spot for a date night. Definitely recommend."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The duck confit was so tender and filling & was my first time trying a calamari with a sweeter sauce - highly recommended. The tuna tartar came with seaweed salad and roasted nori - super delish pairing. The beef tartar was solid too."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "An absolute masterclass in atmosphere and vibes. I go here on a regular basis for the music and late night dancing. The sound system is incredible, and they spin vinyl. A lot of artists frequent here.

As if this already wasn’t enough, they have a delicious lunch/dinner menu cooked by some magical hands.
The pave fries are insane
Staff is super friendly and honestly a well rounded and really cool place to be

One of my favourite spots in the city, hands down"
